tokens: The vesicle population varies from about a millimeter to one centimeter.
It forms about 15 percent of the rock...10 to 15 (percent).
And I've given you grain size for the rocks near the LM and that goes well for this one.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: There is the parting that I mentioned, still of somewhat unknown origin, and we'll try and get a sample along a parting plane.
It's clearly evident in one of the bigger blocks.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Working at Jack's side of the Rover) Hey, Bob, just as we stopped the Rover, I went on Aux(illiary) water.
Do you want me to turn my primary water off?
I don't have to, do I?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Just wanted to cover all bets.
I think, I've got my housekeeping done.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I've been carrying it all day, it's about time I used it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, you're going to want a core at this site?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We'd like to get...Number 1 priority will be some block samples, including any dirt that was on the blocks, if there is such.
And then the second priority is a rake soil sample; the third priority is a double core.
Then, also in there, the pans, of course, and other documented samples.
But the double core is there, although it is third priority.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Gene, do you think...(Pause) Got your gnomon, huh?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yep, I've got my gnomon, and I've got to give them a
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Nothing disrupts your thought patterns more than somebody saying something.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, listen, this is my first geology stop.
I guess I'm entitled to do that; Bob, you ready for a mark?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, you got one (a boulder) picked out?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yes, let's see if we can work on that one.
It's at the edge (of the crater), but we can chip at the parting plane.
And that's one of the things that's come up that I think is of interest; we've got to figure out why they have that foliation in them.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Boy, that rock is one of the more vesicular ones I've seen around.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, they're all about that, Gene.
They're either that or mixed with that variety.
In the same boulder you'll (sometimes) see a non-vesicular....or, relatively non-vesicular (component).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) Ooh, locator's right into the Sun.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Right at that overlapping fracture, huh?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me get where I can maybe save the rock (fragment if it flies off).
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That's what I'm going to do.
I'm going to try and get it right...Right up on top is where I'd like to...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: If you hit it on the right side, it'll go this way, maybe.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) I can get another one, too.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pause) Let me get that one for you.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Can you keep it in sight here for a minute?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Can you use the other end against the right side of the rock?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'll get that one, wait a minute.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: The whole thing is going to fracture off here, in a minute.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me give one more whack.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me get that other piece...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Tipping the first sample out of the scoop into a bag) Okay.
Bag 476 is the rock sample with a little bit of the soil near it.
Of a chip...A chip off the rock, and it's the...Watch it, Gene.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) If I go down there, that thing (the crater) is about 15 feet deep.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Now, do you think you can chip off the other side of that plane, up on the edge?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Then we'll get the soil, and maybe just a small rock, non-chipped.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let me tell you, my hands from that drill...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ...really know I've been out here today.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It's from the southeast side of the parting plane...
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: There it is, a whole big slab, right there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Oh, look at those dark minerals in there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, they may be ilmenite or fresh pyroxene.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, you want my bag (that is, does Jack need to get at Gene's SCB)?
(Pause) I tell you, if you work on any kind of slope, like this little crater...(Pause)
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, I'm going to leave it (Gene's SCB) open for a minute.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm going to have to...You're going to have to use your tongs on that one, I think.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And, 17, a reminder to factor into your thinking, this is only a 30-minute stop, and there's about two-zero (20) minutes remaining.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: But we got to sample something.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Get him (meaning Bob) the bag number, too.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, and the flashes are from inside of vugs and re-crystallized vesicles.
They look like pyroxene flashes; they could be ilmenite.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay; and let me get in there and get some soil.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, the bag tore around that; it's pretty jagged rock, but I think it'll hold.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Having put the sample in Gene's SCB) It's in yours.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: It's in Gene's sample collection bag.
And a scoop sample...(Pause) You got a bag handy, Gene?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Is from the west side of the rock.
It's under a slight overhang of the rock.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Finishing the thought) In a shadow, anyway.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, that's from about 1 centimeter down...1 to 2 centimeters.
And the next one is down to about 5...5 or 6.
(Pause) And it's got some chips in it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Moving out of the field-of-view) Oh, shoot.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: 1.2 kilometers is a long way from the LM.
Look at the Challenger down there.
Makes you get a feel for how big this valley really is.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Turn around and let me help you get these in your bag.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You learn of necessity out here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Putting the sample bags in Jack's SCB) Okay.
See if we can't fill this up for Christmas.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And if we can, we might get just a block instead of breaking on it, and then we'll go to the rake.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Bob wants a core here, too, huh?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, but the rake's next, as you might imagine.
Geno, now this stuff here looks a little less vesicular.
Why don't we try that one?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Hey, look at this rock, where the vesicularity changes from a hummocky vesicularity to a very fine vesicular.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me try and crack...See that?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, that's what I'm after; that's it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let's see if I can't crack...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's what I saw in that other boulder.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Let's see if I can't crack the corner and get that contact.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Or get a piece of both.
I think you can get...if you can reach down there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: See if I can't get a...(Pause)
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's a contact in a rock.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And, do you guys see any 2-meter boulders around there?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, I wish (garbled) get over here and try and knock it...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, if that one showed up in the photos, I wonder why those down near the ALSEP didn't show up.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: No, we're not where you think we are.
We're not sure where we are.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Gene, can you get down into that?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, just give me the shovel to hold myself with.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: See if I can't...It's low and hard to hit.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: How about coming around from this side?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Well, I got the gnomon in the wrong place really.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, I'm going to lean on the rock maybe.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I got that other little piece in sight.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, I got that piece in sight, too.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me get them both right now.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: You can stick that (scoop) in the ground if you...(Pause) (To Houston) Okay, this is a sample of the more coarsely vesicular rock.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You got it in your hand?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I think, actually, we got a sample of both sides; but I wouldn't bet on it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, I just got a chunk of that side.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, I got both of these.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Using the hammer to point to the fragment he dislodged from the east face) See that rock right over there on the little mound, just projecting out of the edge of it?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Moving the scoop a foot eastward) There?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: There you go; you just about touched it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, let me get these in a bag here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Pushing himself upright) And that's the samples from either side of the contact anyway.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Give me the hammer, and get a bag and I'll...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I got these in my hand I want to put there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bag 477 is the coarsely vesicular rock.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I hope two of them fell in.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It had to fall right here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I don't think it ever...There it is; get your tongs.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Now you're full of dirt in the scoop; you just covered it up.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Using the scoop) Got it; I got it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Here, put it in here with the dirt.
A little dirt never hurt anybody.
(Holding the sample bag low) Got it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: They're small, but I think they'll (tell you) if there's any compositional difference.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Preparing to collect the east-face fragment, pointing with the scoop) Are these two are the ones you saw?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, I think you got it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm going to take a close-up stereo on that contact.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay; and, Jack and Gene, when you get done with that boulder, we'd like you to move on to a rake-soil sample, please; and that'll be a kilogram sample, please.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: (Pause) In bag 478 is the chip from the more finely vesicular rock.
It's a small chip; but it'll tell the story, I think.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Giving Jack the scoop) Here you are.
I'll go ahead and get a close-up (garbled)...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Get a close-up, and I'll get the rake.
I'll get started on the rake.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Gene, if you can pick up one more rock in that picture, with your tongs, let's bag it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And, 17; we'd like to have you guys...
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Sure wish I could read this (camera setting).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: That's right, that's right, that's right.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Boy, I can't see my camera (f-stop) setting, it's so full of dust.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I guess you want to sort of (get the rake sample)...out in nothing's land here, huh?
(No answer from Houston) I can bag it for you, Geno.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I want to get this close-up here.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, I've moved about 5 to 8 meters northeast of the Rover.
And as soon as Gene gets here with the gnomon...(Pause)
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Bob, I've got a sample that was laying next to that boulder.
I did not get an "after" picture of it; but, as I was taking my cross-Sun pictures, it is on my side of the boulder just 4 or 5 inches, covered with the dark mantle.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think we probably disturbed that one.
It'll probably show up in the "befores".
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, where do you want a rake.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And that (meaning the extra rock sample)'s in bag 479.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Gene, let's rake right out there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Look, let's go ahead and bag that one (that is, put it in Gene's SCB); and I'll get the gnomon out there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, as you might have seen from the camera, up towards where we think Emory is you get a pretty high concentration of boulders up there.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And I think that that's where we thought we were, a little bit closer to Emory than you are.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Well, we thought about going on up there; although we're in a pretty good area here, too, from the standpoint of boulders.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Again, I think...(To Jack) Facing the Sun.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think for the most part, large and small, all the fragments seem to be filleted or even mantled by the dark material.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Let me get out of your way.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Boy, I've got to clean my camera; I can't even see.
(To Jack) What area are you going to rake?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: To your left of the...Well, ahead of the gnomon and to your left, there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Referring to Gene's remark about the prevalence of filleting) Okay, Gene, we copy that.
And I also gathered that most of the rocks look pretty much the same.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Yeah, except a change in vesicularity...
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: ...in terms of the size of vesicles, where I described one as being a more hummocky vesicular-type rock.
The first time I've noticed any of the dark minerals was when we took that one big, flat chip off that boulder.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I didn't look at it that close to see what it was.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I'm going to get a pan, Jack, while you're doing that.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Man, are there some good targets for the 500(-millimeter camera) around here.
(Pause) We've got to get those Massifs with the 500.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And, Bob, I'm really only penetrating about, at the most, 3 centimeters into this area with the rake.
I've picked up a very good sample of boulders (fragments) but most of them were in that distance of the surface and projecting out of it.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Pause) Okay, coming at you (with a sample bag).
I'll give you a frame count shortly.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Give me a couple of small ones.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: (Getting another bag) Okay, and bag 458 is the rest of the rake sample.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Now we need the kilogram of the soil.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: All the fragments, of course, are completely covered with the mantle (meaning soil); and they are slightly...oh, maybe 20 percent vesicular.
I just took a glance at them.
But, for the most part, they appear to be rounded to subrounded fragments.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, yeah, I can close it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, I just can't even read my camera anymore.
I've got to learn how to control the dust.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I tried to blow the dust off my camera!
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think it's going to be hard to get a double core here.
We could try a single right there.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Bob, we got time to get the core?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We'd like for you to get your second pan, Jack, and then we'll press on.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: I'll get it over here (west of the Rover) where our two sample sites are in view.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (Looking toward the northwest) Well, now I know why I felt that we were much too close to Trident than what I thought.
We weren't really too close to Trident, because Trident is way out here.
A guy would know if he landed 100 meters from a big set of craters like that.
(Pause; going to the Rover) You know, on a landing site like this, you ought to know exactly where you are.
(Pause) Anyway, I landed where I wanted to.
(Pause) Okay, Bob, here's a (gravimeter) reading for you (as per
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, and no more (seismic) charges to deploy going back, right?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: No, we will deploy charge number 7 on the way back.
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: It will be deployed at the same location we were originally planning on deploying it, which was in the checklist (on
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And, Jack, you got the pan or getting it?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: And, Bob, CDR is on frame count 60.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And the LMP is on nine-five.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: And the rake and the scoop?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The rake and the scoop are back on (the gate).
I'll set the low gain (antenna), and we'll be ready to do it.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Bob, my impression right now is that the dark mantle may just be a...Well, at least in here, it's indistinguishable from a regolith that might be derived from these other rocks.
It seems to be a little dark for that, but that might be the answer.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: We hope to do (more) on that (dark mantle question) again tomorrow.
And EP-7 is the charge, right?
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: Jack, I figured out (that) if you mount the Rover at 90 degrees, when you kick up your feet, you'll miss the dust.
(Pause) Let me hook you in before I do that.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Just put your feet 90 degrees to it (the Rover).
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I hope my bag was closed.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Yeah, as a matter of fact, I did.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: You know, you ought to put that sampler (garbled)...(Answering Bob) Not yet.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: It's not the sampler; it's these bags with memory.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: This thing is too high for you.
You're hitting it all the time.
(To Bob) No, we're not on our way, Bob.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: And you want the charge deployed at 320/0.7 (as per the fourth line from the bottom of
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: We'll change that to 0.6 on EP-7; and it will really be just wherever you cross 0.6 on the range (that is, at about 650 meters from the SEP.)
transcript.speaker: Parker

tokens: That took out some of your (length)...Wait a minute.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Your left hand's in the way now.
Twist it away from you 180 degrees.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Well, let me see...You got it?
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: I think we're learning, (and) that's half of the first EVA.
transcript.speaker: Cernan

tokens: Okay, copy that, Jack...(correcting himself) Gene.
transcript.speaker: Parker
